channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Flock Cameras Are Being Used for Stalking
item.description: <p>There are over a dozen cases around the country where police officers are using the Flock surveillance camera system to obsessively and illegally <a href="https://www.404media.co/cops-keep-getting-arrested-for-using-flock-to-stalk-people/">stalk people</a>.</p> <p><a href="https://archive.ph/l5KcH">Alternate link</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Chainguard, JPMorgan, BNY Team Up to Secure Open Source from AI Threats
item.description: Athena is a new an industry coalition to fix the vulnerabilities frontier AI models find before attackers can exploit them

item.title: Atomic Arch Supply Chain Attack Hits 1,500 AUR Packages
item.description: <p>Arch Linux suspended account registrations in response to the wave of malicious packages being uploaded to AUR.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/atomic-arch-supply-chain-attack-hits-1500-aur-packages/">Atomic Arch Supply Chain Attack Hits 1,500 AUR Packages</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Attackers Exploit Three Fortinet FortiSandbox Flaws, One Patched Last Week
item.description:
Bad actors are exploiting multiple security vulnerabilities in Fortinet FortiSandbox, according to threat intelligence firm Defused Cyber.
In a post shared on X, the company said it has observed exploitation of CVE-2026-39813, CVE-2026-39808, and CVE-2026-25089 over the past 24 hours.
CVE-2026-39813 (CVSS score: 9.1) refers to a path traversal vulnerability in FortiSandbox JRPC API that could
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cybersecurity Executives Urge the Trump Administration to Ease Restrictions on Anthropic AI Models
item.description: <p>A group of cybersecurity executives and experts is asking the Trump administration to lift its directive preventing the use of Anthropic’s latest artificial intelligence models by foreign nationals.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/cybersecurity-executives-urge-the-trump-administration-to-ease-restrictions-on-anthropic-ai-models/">Cybersecurity Executives Urge the Trump Administration to Ease Restrictions on Anthropic AI Models</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Associated Press

item.title: China-Linked SprySOCKS Backdoor Expands to Windows with Driver-Based Stealth
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged two previously undocumented Windows variants of what was believed to be a Linux-only backdoor called SprySOCKS.
"The Windows variants discovered are internally marked as WIN_DRV and WIN_PLUS," ESET said in a report shared with The Hacker News. "Both come with a hard-coded C&amp;C [command-and-control] configuration and support communication over TCP, UDP,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Tech Coalition ‘Athena’ Targets OSS Vulnerabilities Ahead of Disclosure
item.description: <p>Over two dozen organizations built a shared platform to triage vulnerabilities, fix them, and secure the software before patches arrive.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/tech-coalition-athena-targets-oss-vulnerabilities-ahead-of-disclosure/">Tech Coalition &#8216;Athena&#8217; Targets OSS Vulnerabilities Ahead of Disclosure</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: FBI Warns Courier Cash Pickups Are Driving Crypto Scams
item.description: The FBI claims couriers are being used to circumvent bank transfers in crypto investment schemes

item.title: Fake Microsoft Alerts Used to Deploy North Korean NarwhalRAT Malware
item.description:
The North Korean state-sponsored hacking group known as ScarCruft (aka APT37) has been observed using spear-phishing messages impersonating Microsoft Account security notifications to deliver malware called NarwhalRAT.
"The attack email contained a message impersonating an MS account security alert," the Genians Security Center (GSC) said. "It was designed to create concern over possible
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cisco Patches Another SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited in Attacks
item.description: <p>Cisco recently became aware of the exploitation of CVE-2026-20262, a Catalyst SD-WAN Manager zero-day that allows arbitrary file write.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/cisco-patches-another-sd-wan-zero-day-exploited-in-attacks/">Cisco Patches Another SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited in Attacks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Cisco Releases Security Updates for Actively Exploited SD-WAN Manager Flaw
item.description:
Cisco has released security updates for a medium-severity security flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager that has come under active exploitation in the wild.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20262, carries a CVSS score of 6.5 out of 10.0.
"A vulnerability in the web UI of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, formerly SD-WAN vManage, could allow an authenticated, remote attacker to create a file or
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Flags LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Flaw Exploited for Root Privilege Escalation
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a security flaw impacting LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to apply the fixes by June 18, 2026.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-54420 (CVSS score: 8.5), which has been described as a case of privilege
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Nintendo Alleged Data Breach: Threat Actor Demands $2M Ransom
item.description: <p>Nintendo faces an alleged data extortion incident involving HR records, internal reports, and potential exposure of third-party vendors.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-nintendo-alleged-data-leak-third-party-risk/">Nintendo Alleged Data Breach: Threat Actor Demands $2M Ransom</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Chinese Hackers Abused Google Workspace Rules to Steal Research and Defense Emails
item.description:
A China-linked espionage group hid inside North American medical, academic, and military research networks for more than a year, quietly stealing sensitive research and defense email.
The way in was a backdoor on their REDCap research servers that stole login credentials. The exfiltration was the unusual part: the attackers rewired the victims' own Google Workspace rules to copy any message
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: North Korean Hackers Are Turning Developer Tools Into Malware Delivery Channels
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged two malicious cyber campaigns that exhibit similarities with a persistent North Korean threat cluster known as Contagious Interview (aka Famous Chollima, HexagonalRodent, and Void Dokkaebi).
According to a report published by Proofpoint, the threat actor has been found orchestrating phishing campaigns using developer role recruitment or code review themes
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Attackers Hijack Popular WordPress Plugins to Deploy Backdoors
item.description: Tampered OptinMonster and sister plugins plant hidden backdoors on 1.2 million WordPress sites

item.title: LiteLLM Vulnerability Chain Lets Low-Privilege Users Take Over AI Gateway Servers
item.description:
A default low-privilege account on a LiteLLM proxy can climb to full admin and run code on the server by chaining three vulnerabilities, researchers at Obsidian Security disclosed
LiteLLM is a widely deployed open-source AI gateway that brokers calls to more than 100 model providers behind one OpenAI-compatible interface.
A server takeover exposes every provider key it holds, the secrets that
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Adriatic Port Cyber-Attack by Anubis Sparks Warning Over Maritime Security Risks
item.description: How the Anubis ransomware group stole and leaked an Italian Adriatic port authority's data

item.title: Ransomware Attack Shuts Down Mills of Australia’s Second-Largest Sugar Producer
item.description: <p>Mackay Sugar was targeted in a cyberattack carried out by a threat group known as The Gentlemen.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/ransomware-attack-shuts-down-mills-of-australias-second-largest-sugar-producer/">Ransomware Attack Shuts Down Mills of Australia&#8217;s Second-Largest Sugar Producer</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: One-Click Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Steal Emails, Files, and MFA Codes
item.description:
A single click on a trusted Microsoft link could have let an attacker pull emails, calendar details, and indexed files out of Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search.
Researchers at Varonis Threat Labs chained three bugs into a one-click exfiltration path they call SearchLeak. Because the link pointed to a real microsoft.com domain, traditional anti-phishing and URL filtering tools were
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Chinese Hackers Target Medical, Military, and AI Research in North America
item.description: <p>Google’s Threat Intelligence Group has been tracking the cyberespionage group as UNC6508 since early 2025.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/chinese-hackers-target-medical-military-and-ai-research-in-north-america/">Chinese Hackers Target Medical, Military, and AI Research in North America</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Google Bug Hunter Claims $500K From AI-Assisted Vulnerability Pipeline
item.description: <p>A researcher known as Brutecat says an AI-assisted pipeline helped map and test more than 1,500 Google APIs. Google has not confirmed the $500,000 payout total.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-ai-bug-bounty-pipeline/">Google Bug Hunter Claims $500K From AI-Assisted Vulnerability Pipeline</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Chrome 0-Day, UniFi Exploits, macOS Stealers, VPN Flaw and More
item.description:
Stuff broke again. Not in a movie way. An old tool was left exposed. An abandoned package was abused. A deprecated feature was still running in prod.
This week is the same lesson in a new form: phishing kits are easier to rent, AI names are useful bait, old login paths still fail, and forgotten software keeps becoming someone else's entry point.
Scroll through the full Monday Cybersecurity
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Oracle Warns PeopleSoft Customers After Critical Zero-Day Exploited
item.description: <p>Oracle issued emergency guidance for CVE-2026-35273, a critical PeopleSoft flaw exploited in a ShinyHunters-linked campaign targeting universities.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-oracle-peoplesoft-zero-day-shinyhunters/">Oracle Warns PeopleSoft Customers After Critical Zero-Day Exploited</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: NewCore Emerges From Stealth Mode With $66 Million in Funding
item.description: <p>The startup has built a security-first identity platform to protect humans, machines, and AI agents.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/newcore-emerges-from-stealth-mode-with-66-million-in-funding/">NewCore Emerges From Stealth Mode With $66 Million in Funding</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges
item.description: <p>Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko admitted to working on the development of a loader for the Conti gang.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/ukrainian-man-pleads-guilty-in-us-to-conti-ransomware-charges/">Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: The Onboarding Password Mistake That Creates Unnecessary Risk
item.description:
Employee onboarding is a busy time for IT teams. New starters need devices, accounts, access permissions, and passwords, all delivered within a tight timeframe.
That usually means sharing a temporary "first-day" password so employees can access systems for the first time. The issue is that these passwords don't always stay temporary. They may be sent over email or SMS, reused across accounts,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems
item.description: <p>The pharmaceutical giant says the attackers gained access to personal data stored on the compromised systems. </p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/ozempic-maker-novo-nordisk-says-hackers-breached-it-systems/">Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: French Government Messaging Platform Breached by Mysterious ‘Misere’ Hacker
item.description: <p>French officials say roughly 73,000 government accounts were affected, while the threat actor claims to have stolen messages and user data from the sovereign Tchap platform.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/french-government-messaging-platform-breached-by-mysterious-misere-hacker/">French Government Messaging Platform Breached by Mysterious ‘Misere’ Hacker</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend

item.title: 152 Chrome Wallpaper Extensions with 105K Installs Linked to Adware and Fake Traffic
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a network of 152 Google Chrome extensions that act as new tab live wallpaper add-ons to distribute a potentially unwanted program (PUP) family.
The cluster spans 38 separate Chrome Web Store publisher accounts and three brand backends: tabplugins[.]com, yowgames[.]com, and chromewallpaper[.]com. They have been collectively installed 105,000 times. The
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The FCC Wants to Eliminate Burner Phones
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Cybersecurity Experts Urge US to Lift Ban on Anthropic's Frontier AI Models
item.description: Access to two Anthropic large language models, Mythos 5 and Fable 5, has effectively been banned to any non-US nationals by the Trump administration

item.title: Popular WordPress Plugin Scripts Tampered to Plant Hidden Backdoors on Sites
item.description:
An attacker tampered with trusted JavaScript files used by WordPress sites running PushEngage, OptinMonster, and TrustPulse, turning those files into a way to break into the sites.
When a site administrator was logged in as the file loaded, the code created an admin account under the attacker's control and installed a hidden plugin that opened a way back in. Ordinary visitors did not trigger it
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: UK Government Finds 400+ Vulnerabilities in AI Hackathons
item.description: Government departments find hundreds of vulnerabilities after testing frontier models

item.title: Maine Takes Breach Reporting Portal Offline After Fake Entries
item.description: The Office of the Maine Attorney General has suspended its breach reporting portal

item.title: EvilTokens: A phishing attack that doesn’t steal your password
item.description: A phishing kit subverting Microsoft’s legitimate authentication flow lets attackers break into accounts without stealing passwords or creating fake login pages

item.title: Sniper Dz Scams Target MENA Users via Fake Facebook Offers and Browser Alerts
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of fraudulent activity targeting users across the Middle East and North Africa by employing various fraudulent Facebook accounts impersonating politicians, public figures, and trusted organizations.
"These accounts promoted fake offers, including free mobile internet packages, financial compensation, and government subsidy programs," Group-IB
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Palo Alto Warns of Active Exploitation of PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN Flaw
item.description:
Palo Alto Networks has revealed that it has observed "active exploitation" of a recently disclosed PAN-OS vulnerability by an unknown threat actor to obtain unauthorized access to GlobalProtect portals.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), an authentication bypass flaw affecting the portal and gateway components of PAN-OS software that could be exploited by bad
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
item.description:
Splunk has released security updates to address a critical security flaw in Splunk Enterprise that could be exploited to conduct unauthenticated file operations and even remote code execution.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20253, is rated 9.8 on the CVSS scoring system.
"In Splunk Enterprise versions below 10.2.4 and 10.0.7, an unauthenticated user could create or truncate arbitrary
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
item.description:
Anthropic said on Friday it will "abruptly disable" its most advanced artificial intelligence (AI) models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users after the U.S. government ordered it to suspend access to the models for foreign nationals, whether inside or outside the U.S., citing national security concerns.
The AI company said it received an order at 5:21 p.m. ET, instructing it to suspend
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid-Inspired Fluid Pump
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: New Windows Zero-Day Claims BitLocker Bypass Amid Microsoft Disclosure Fight
item.description: <p>A new Windows zero-day reportedly bypasses BitLocker, adding pressure on Microsoft as researchers debate the exploit’s real-world impact.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-windows-bitlocker-zero-day-june-2026/">New Windows Zero-Day Claims BitLocker Bypass Amid Microsoft Disclosure Fight</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Matt Gonzales

item.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
item.description:
Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them.
The malware is a Rust binary built to harvest developer secrets. When it lands with root, it can also load an eBPF rootkit to hide itself. The AUR is Arch Linux's community package collection, and it is separate
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
item.description:
Google on Friday said it's pursuing legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network, accusing it of using its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) agent to send phishing text messages targeting Americans.
The network is said to be behind the development and management of a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) software kit called Outsider, per the tech giant.
"The operation weaponized Gemini to help
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
item.description:
Instead of hiding on the laptops and servers defenders watch most closely, a China-nexus group spent close to a decade hidden inside the Linux login system itself.
Sygnia, which tracks the group as Velvet Ant, says it backdoored the PAM and OpenSSH components that decide who is allowed to sign in, planting its access where ordinary cleanup could not reach it. The network it targeted had no
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Rivalries, Cyberthreats, and IPO Fever Define This Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from June 8–12.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-rivalries-cyberthreats-and-ipo-fever-define-this-week-in-tech/">AI Rivalries, Cyberthreats, and IPO Fever Define This Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Ransomware Payment Crypto Laundering Platform Taken Out by FBI and Europol
item.description: Domain of dark web money laundering platform AudiA6 seized and suspects arrested in joint operation by the FBI, Europol and others

item.title: GitHub to Update npm to Thwart Software Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: NPM, part of GitHub, announced a new version of the npm package manager with several security improvements, including disabling install scripts

item.title: Agentjacking Attack Tricks AI Coding Agents Into Running Malicious Code
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have described what they say is a new class of attack that can trick artificial intelligence (AI) coding agents into running arbitrary code on developer machines.
Called Agentjacking by Tenet Security, the attack can be triggered by means of a fake error report crafted using Sentry, an open-source error-tracking and performance-monitoring platform.
"The attack
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Bernie Sanders’ AI Sovereign Wealth Fund Plan
item.description: <p>Let no one accuse Bernie Sanders of ducking the big questions. Writing in the New York Times last week, the senator <a href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/01/opinion/artificial-intelligence-bernie-sanders.html">asked</a>: &#8220;Will the future of humanity be determined by a handful of billionaires who have promoted and developed AI, with virtually no democratic input, who stand to become even richer and more powerful than they are today?&#8221;</p> <p>We agree entirely that this is one of the most potent questions facing global democracy today. Our book, <a href="https://mitpress.mit.edu/9780262049948/rewiring-democracy/">Rewiring Democracy</a>, surveys the emerging uses for and impacts of AI in democracy around the world and reaches the same conclusion: that the most urgent risk posed by AI is the ...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Rethinking MDR as Attackers and Defenders Embrace AI
item.description:
For most of the past decade, managed detection and response was the answer to a real problem. Security teams couldn't staff around the clock, couldn't hire enough analysts, and needed someone else to handle the alert queue. MDR stepped in. It worked well enough. Until now.
The threat landscape has changed faster than the MDR model can adapt. Attackers are using AI to move faster, generate more
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Over 80% of Sports Organizations Targeted by Hackers in the Last Year
item.description: As the FIFA World Cup 2026 kicks off, a new Darktrace report warns that sports teams and bodies are a major target for cyber criminals

item.title: LangGraph Flaw Chain Exposes Self-Hosted AI Agents to Remote Code Execution
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of three now-patched security flaws impacting LangGraph, including a critical vulnerability chain that could result in remote code execution.
LangGraph is an open-source framework created by LangChain to build complex, stateful, and multi-agent artificial intelligence (AI) agentic applications.
"An SQL injection in LangGraph's function could
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: INTERPOL Operation Takes Down Sniper Dz Phishing Platform, Arrests Administrator
item.description:
An INTERPOL-led operation last month resulted in the disruption of Sniper Dz, a decade-long phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform, Group-IB said Thursday.
The effort, codenamed Operation Ramz, took place between October 2025 and February 2026, and saw authorities from 13 countries in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region making 201 arrests.
Included among them was Guedz, the primary
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Europol Disrupts AudiA6 Crypto Laundering Service Used by Ransomware Gangs
item.description:
Authorities in Europe have disrupted AudiA6, a cryptocurrency laundering service used by ransomware gangs and cybercriminal networks.
Europol, in a statement issued Thursday, said the dismantling of AudiA6 cut off a "key financial pipeline used to wash hundreds of millions in illicit profits." The service is estimated to have been used to launder more than €336 million (~$389 million) since the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day (CVE-2026-35273) to Breach Universities
item.description:
The ShinyHunters extortion crew exploited an unpatched flaw in Oracle PeopleSoft to break into enterprise systems, steal data, and demand payment to keep it private. The campaign hit universities hardest.
Google's Mandiant attributes it to the group it tracks as UNC6240, and dates the activity between May 27 and June 9. Oracle did not publish its advisory until June 10, so the bug was a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New Attacks Trick OpenClaw AI Agent Into Running Code and Leaking Secrets
item.description:
Two security teams have shown, in separate research published this week, that OpenClaw, the popular self-hosted AI agent, can be driven to run attacker-controlled code or hand over sensitive data through ordinary-looking inputs.
Imperva buried instructions inside shared contacts, vCards, and location pins that the agent executed without the victim ever seeing them. Varonis built a test agent on
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New GreatXML Exploit Bypasses Windows BitLocker via Recovery Partition XML Files
item.description:
Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse and MSNightmare) has released a new Windows BitLocker bypass dubbed GreatXML, a day after they published an exploit for Microsoft Defender.
"This was an accidental discovery, it took a total of 4 hours to find this," the researcher said in a post on Blogger. "If you ever attempted to use Windows Defender Offline Scan, you're
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Gentlemen Ransomware Claims 478 Victims, Can Spread Like a Worm
item.description:
A new analysis of The Gentlemen operation has revealed that the financially motivated threat group initially operated as an affiliate responsible for conducting double extortion attacks, while leveraging resources from various ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) schemes like LockBit (aka Tenacious Mantis), Qilin (aka Pestilent Mantis), and Medusa (aka Venomous Mantis).
According to a detailed report
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: South Korea Drops a $409M Fine on Coupang in Historic Data Breach Ruling
item.description: <p>South Korea fined Coupang $409 million after regulators said weak security controls led to a massive breach affecting 37.5 million accounts.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-coupang-record-fine-409m-apac-south-korea/">South Korea Drops a $409M Fine on Coupang in Historic Data Breach Ruling</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Microsoft Restricts Claude Fable 5 Access Amid AI Safety Review
item.description: <p>Microsoft reportedly limited internal use of Claude Fable 5 while legal teams review Anthropic’s 30-day data-retention policy.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-claude-fable-5-data-retention/">Microsoft Restricts Claude Fable 5 Access Amid AI Safety Review</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: CISA Warning: LiteLLM Flaw Could Expose Enterprise AI Gateways
item.description: <p>CISA’s LiteLLM warning shows why AI gateways and agents need service account governance, scoped access, credential rotation, and audit trails.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-litellm-cisa-ai-gateway-service-account-governance/">CISA Warning: LiteLLM Flaw Could Expose Enterprise AI Gateways</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Tim Freestone

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring SecurityMetrics
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: CISA Orders Agencies to Patch by Risk, Not Severity
item.description: New CISA directive tells federal agencies to patch by real-world risk, not CVSS severity scores

item.title: Cybercriminals Use Fake AI Guides and Dev Tools to Spread AsyncRAT Malware
item.description: Fake AI guides hide a multi-stage chain that drops AsyncRAT, with signs of AI-assisted coding

item.title: Cybersecurity Stars Awards 2026: Winners Announced Across 95 Categories
item.description:
Most good security work is invisible by design. Today is the exception.
The 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards winners are announced across 95 subcategories in four main award categories.
The reason is simple. Cybersecurity is full of work that deserves recognition and rarely gets it. Products that quietly close real gaps. Teams that stop incidents nobody reads about. Companies that raise the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Worm Code Leaked, AI Agent Phished, Claude Code Patch + 28 New Stories
item.description:
It's been one of those weeks. You expect the usual noise: recycled malware, sloppy attacks, another easy target getting hit. Instead, there's a supply chain attack kit in a public repo, a $5,000-a-month RAT that clones browsers, and research showing AI agents can be tricked into leaking real credentials.
The bigger problem is how polished this all looks now. Mule networks run like SaaS.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Most Cybersecurity Teams Struggle to Find Time for Training on New Cyber Threats
item.description: Organizations are aware of the challenges that new technologies like AI bring: but cybersecurity staff struggle to make time for the required training during working hours

item.title: India’s AI Hardware Scrutiny Puts Biometric Devices in Focus
item.description: <p>India’s data-protection framework is moving into phased implementation while officials turn their attention to AI-linked hardware, biometric devices, and trusted-source controls.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-ai-biometric-hardware-controls-apac-india/">India’s AI Hardware Scrutiny Puts Biometric Devices in Focus</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: AI Broke Vulnerability Management. That's Why CISOs Are Moving Budget to BAS.
item.description:
For thirty years, vulnerability management ran on a buffer: the months between when a vulnerability was found and when someone could figure out how to weaponize it. The solution was straightforward enough; triage by severity, schedule the fix, validate, and move on. The buffer was what made that work.
Today, that buffer is gone.
AI didn't make your team slower. It changed the other side of the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Interpol Dismantles SniperDz Phishing-as-a-Service Platform
item.description: New revelations by Group-IB expose the full scale of the decade-old SniperDz phishing operation

item.title: Enhanced License Plate Tracking
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Extortion-Only Attacks Increase, With Data Theft Dominating Ransomware Claims
item.description: Extortion-only attacks are increasing as data theft drives most ransomware claims, with many organizations unable to stop stolen data from being exposed

item.title: OceanLotus Hits Vietnam Investors With SPECTRALVIPER in FireAnt Attack
item.description:
The Vietnam-aligned threat actor known as OceanLotus has been attributed to two distinct campaigns that targeted domestic entities and stock investors with a backdoor known as SPECTRALVIPER.
The campaigns involve a prolonged cyber espionage operation aimed at a Vietnamese infrastructure and transport construction corporation between mid-2024 and February 2026, as well as a supply chain attack
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New “Agentjacking” Attacks Could Hijack AI Coding Agents
item.description: Tenet Security researchers reveal how new “agentjacking” attacks could trick coding agents into executing arbitrary code

item.title: OceanLotus: From external espionage to domestic targeting
item.description: A shift in operational pattern of the infamous Vietnam-aligned APT group

item.title: GitHub to Disable npm Install Scripts by Default to Stop Supply Chain Attacks
item.description:
GitHub has announced what it said are "breaking changes" coming to npm version 12, one of which turns off install scripts by default to combat software supply chain threats.
The changes aim to combat attack techniques that abuse the "npm install" command to trigger the execution of malicious code using npm lifecycle hooks. "Npm install" is used to download and install all the necessary
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: PCI SSC Publishes New Guidance on Compensating Controls and the Customized Approach
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: China-Linked JDY Botnet Expands to 1,500+ Devices for Cyber Reconnaissance
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a "resurgence and expansion" of JDY, a covert network associated with China-nexus state-sponsored threat actors.
"The JDY botnet comprises over 1,500 SOHO [small office and home office] and IoT devices and operates as a centrally controlled, high-performance scanner used to discover, fingerprint, and continuously map exposed services at scale," Lumen's
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fake Software Tutorials on TikTok Spread Vidar Stealer
item.description: Threat actors push fake free-software tutorials on TikTok and Instagram to spread Vidar stealer

item.title: New SilabRAT Trojan Hijacks Sessions to Steal Crypto
item.description: MaaS trojan SilabRAT uses HVNC and browser cloning to hijack sessions and steal crypto

item.title: Cybersecurity Software Fails to Detect Fifth of Brower-Based Phishing Attacks
item.description: Menlo Security research warns that as enterprise applications become increasingly browser based, traditional cybersecurity tools leave them vulnerable to cyber threats

item.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, and SAP Release Patches for Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Fortinet, Ivanti, and SAP have released security updates to address multiple critical security vulnerabilities that could result in arbitrary code execution and information disclosure.
The security flaw patched by Fortinet relates to a command injection vulnerability in FortiSandbox, FortiSandbox Cloud, and FortiSandbox PaaS WEB UI. It's tracked as CVE-2026-25089 (CVSS score: 9.1).
"An
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Langflow Vulnerability CVE-2026-5027 Exploited for Unauthenticated RCE
item.description:
A high-severity security flaw in Langflow, an open-source low-code platform to build artificial intelligence (AI) applications, has come under active exploitation in the wild, according to findings from VulnCheck.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-5027 (CVSS score: 8.8), a case of path traversal that could allow an attacker to write files to arbitrary locations.
"The 'POST /api/v2/
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Adds Cisco, Chrome, and Arista Flaws to KEV Catalog Amid Active Exploitation
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added three new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of active exploitation.
The list of vulnerabilities is as follows -
CVE-2026-20245 (CVSS score: 7.8) - An improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager that could allow an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Microsoft’s Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday Fixes Over 200 Security Flaws
item.description: <p>Microsoft’s June Patch Tuesday fixed about 200 CVEs, including public zero-days and Critical Windows flaws tied to patching pressure.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-june-patch-tuesday-record-breaking/">Microsoft’s Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday Fixes Over 200 Security Flaws</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: NSO Group Hacking WhatsApp Despite Court Order
item.description: <p>WhatsApp has <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/whatsapp-catches-spyware-firm-nso-defying-no-hacking-court-order/">caught</a> the NSO Group phishing its users, in violation of a court order.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: New Fable 5 Is a "Mythos-Class" LLM Available to All, Anthropic Announces
item.description: Anthropic unveils Claude Mythos 5 and Fable 5, a restricted-access frontier AI model and guardrailed version for everyone to use

item.title: Your Automated Pentest Looks Clean. See What It Missed in This Expert Webinar
item.description:
Your pentest report looks clean. That might be the problem.
Run automated pentesting long enough, and the new findings start to dry up. By the third or fourth run, fewer issues appear. The report looks stable. Leadership reads "stable" as "secure." It usually isn't. The work slows down. The risk does not.
That gap is what a The Hacker News webinar with Picus Security sets out to close.
Autumn
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Over a Quarter of Identity Crime Victims Hit by Multiple Incidents, ITRC Data Shows
item.description: Nearly 26% of identity crime victims faced multiple incidents in the past year, as ITRC warns of a growing "multi-layered crisis"

item.title: Microsoft Patches Record 206 Flaws, Including Three Zero-Days and Critical RCE Bugs
item.description:
Microsoft on Tuesday released fixes for a record 206 security vulnerabilities impacting its software portfolio, including three flaws that have been publicly disclosed at the time of release.
Of the 206 flaws, 39 are rated Critical, and 167 are rated Important in severity. This includes 63 privilege escalation, 56 remote code execution, 30 information disclosure, 27 spoofing, 20 security
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Unpacking SMB cyber-readiness – and what makes or breaks it
item.description: A company that's expecting a cyberattack but hasn’t actively prepared for it risks making the hardest decisions at the worst possible moment

item.title: Microsoft Fixes 200 CVEs in June Patch Tuesday
item.description: Microsoft has patched 200 vulnerabilities including three zero-days

item.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Fable 5, Its Most Powerful AI Yet, With Cyber Safeguards
item.description:
On June 9, Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, the most capable model it has ever made, generally available. It also did something unusual: it shipped one model as two products, split not by capability but by a layer of safety classifiers.
Fable 5 goes to the public. Its twin, Claude Mythos 5, the same underlying model with the cyber safeguards lifted, stays locked to a vetted group of cyber
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ServiceNow Flaw Exploited to Gain Unauthorized Access to Customer Instances
item.description:
ServiceNow has warned about a security incident in which unknown threat actors exploited a flaw to obtain deeper unauthorized access to susceptible instances.
"On June 5, 2026, ServiceNow applied a security update to hosted customer instances," the company revealed in an advisory that requires customer access. "The update concerned a security issue that could allow an unauthenticated user, in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Defender RoguePlanet Zero-Day Grants SYSTEM Access on Updated Windows
item.description:
The anonymous security researcher going by the name Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) has released a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit for yet another Microsoft Defender zero-day named RoguePlanet.
"The exploit is a race condition, so it's a hit or miss," the researcher, who published the exploit under a new GitHub account "MSNightmare" said. "I have managed to get a 100% success rate on
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Six Proto6 Vulnerabilities in protobuf.js Expose Node.js Apps to RCE and DoS
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged half a dozen vulnerabilities in protobuf.js, a JavaScript and TypeScript implementation of Protocol Buffers (Protobuf), that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution (RCE) and denial-of-service (DoS) attacks.
"In affected environments, a single malicious protobuf schema, descriptor, or crafted payload could be enough to trigger
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
item.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs
